channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: GOP's immigration pitch to New Jersey Latinos aims for some distance from Trump in governor’s race
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — At a Spanish-speaking town hall, the Republican candidate for New Jersey governor assured two young Mexican immigrants who were brought to the United States as children that people without criminal records would not face deportation.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Live updates as 'No Kings’ protests underway
item.description: The rallies are taking place during the government shutdown as well federal troop deployments across the country. Follow this story for live updates.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's immigration crackdown weighs heavy on the US labor market
item.description: President Donald Trump’s sweeping crackdown on immigration is throwing foreigners out of work and shaking the American economy and job market.
item.dc:creator: Paul Wiseman And Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: REBECCA GRANT: How Tomahawks work and how they could change everything for Ukraine
item.description: Trump weighs sending 1,000-mile range Tomahawk cruise missiles to Ukraine, which could target Russian airbases and energy infrastructure across Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: San Diegans host rally for Ukraine following President Trump's meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: President Donald Trump met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy at the White House today to discuss ending the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Dominique LaVigne, Fox 5

item.title: No SNAP benefits to be issued in November as government shutdown stretches on
item.description: Local governments are warning citizens that because the federal government has been shut down for weeks with no end in sight, November SNAP benefits will not be issued.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Trump offers US automakers additional relief from his tariffs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is giving domestic automakers additional relief from tariffs on auto parts, extending what was supposed to have been a short-term rebate until 2030.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US has seized survivors after strike on suspected drug-carrying vessel in Caribbean, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States took survivors into custody after the military struck a suspected drug-carrying vessel in the Caribbean, a defense official and another person familiar with the matter said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From bills to berets: Notable moments from NYC's contentious mayoral debate
item.description: New York City's mayoral candidates duked it out in their first general election debate Thursday night, with contenders Zohran Mamdani, Andrew Cuomo and Curtis Sliwa sparring over everything from the Israel-Hamas war to the city's education system.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Bolton pleads not guilty
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DeSantis calls for tax relief; House unveils 8 proposals
item.description: Florida's House Speaker Danny Perez dropped eight new property tax proposals for the legislature to consider next session.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump drops f-bomb in Zelensky meeting about Maduro
item.description: "He doesn't want to f‑‑‑ with the U.S.," Trump said, speaking from the White House during a lunch meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: New York GOP shuts down state's Young Republicans over group chat
item.description: The New York Republican State Committee’s leaders unanimously voted to disband the Empire State’s Young Republicans chapter on Friday after screenshots from a Telegram chat exposed members using antisemitic and racist language.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Obama, aiming to boost Democrats in New Jersey and Virginia, endorses Sherrill and Spanberger
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — Barack Obama has endorsed the Democratic candidates for governor in New Jersey and Virginia, seeking to boost Mikie Sherrill and Abigail Spanberger ahead of high-stakes elections next month.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: St. Paul mayoral candidates lay out their visions for Minnesota's capital city
item.description: Three candidates for St. Paul mayor discuss key issues in a MPR News and Pioneer Press debate. Incumbent Melvin Carter, state Rep. Kaohly Her and scientist Yan Chen address city spending, taxes, amenities and public safety.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Jeff Jones, MPR News

item.title: Democrats say Trump needs to be involved in shutdown talks. He's shown little interest in doing so
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is showing little urgency to broker a compromise that would end the government shutdown, even as Democrats insist no breakthrough is possible without his direct involvement. Three weeks in, Congress is at a standstill.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Increased US pressure on Venezuela raises specter of regime change
item.description: President Trump's authorization of covert CIA strikes in Venezuela raises concerns of regime change and potential blowback from Caracas.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Thune to force Democrats to vote on paying the troops during the shutdown
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) plans to force Democrats to vote next week on legislation to pay more than 1 million military service members during the government shutdown, doubling down on his strategy of highlighting the plight of military families, according to Senate GOP sources.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: John Bolton, ex-Trump adviser, pleads not guilty to federal charges
item.description: Bolton, a former adviser to President Trump, is accused of storing and sharing classified information and could face years in prison if convicted.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariffs hurting New Mexicans, Democrat says
item.description: SANTA TERESA, New Mexico (Border Report) – As a former secretary of the Interior during the Biden administration, Debra Haaland oversaw wildlife agencies and the conservation of public lands.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis mayoral candidates push for limits on ICE
item.description: Three challengers for the Minneapolis mayor’s seat say they support a ban on Minneapolis police sharing information with federal immigration agencies or providing crowd control for agencies like ICE.
item.dc:creator: Jon Collins, MPR News

item.title: Megyn Kelly says she'd run for president to stop Ocasio-Cortez
item.description: Conservative pundit Megyn Kelly said Thursday she could be motivated to run for president if Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) made her own White House bid. Laying into the progressive lawmaker on her popular podcast and radio show on Thursday, Kelly exclaimed "eff that woman.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the colleges rejecting Trump's funding compact
item.description: Multiple colleges are rejecting a compact the Trump administration sent to nine universities at the beginning of October that guaranteed funding advantages if the institutions agreed to certain policy changes.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Florida House reveals property tax reduction proposals
item.description: The Florida House of Representatives revealed several proposals for property tax reductions on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: ICE arrests Illinois officer with expired visa
item.description: Bojovic overstayed a B-2 tourist visa that required him to leave the country by March 31, 2015, according to ICE officials.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to meet Putin in Hungary in effort to end 'inglorious' war
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he intends to meet with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Hungary in the coming weeks to discuss efforts to end the war in Ukraine. Trump made the announcement following a lengthy phone call with Putin.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: They tried to self-deport, then got stranded
item.description: The Trump administration’s immigration crackdown has left many immigrants afraid of being detained and deported. Some have opted to leave the U.S. on their own. The Trump administration’s immigration crackdown has left many immigrants afraid of being detained and deported.
item.dc:creator: ProPublica Staff, MPR News

item.title: The shutdown is good for business for flood insurance
item.description: With the government shutdown, the National Flood Insurance Program is no longer writing new policies. It's causing problems for would-be homeowners, but private companies have stepped in to help.
item.dc:creator: Greg Allen, MPR News

item.title: Florida lawmakers revisit medical malpractice bill
item.description: It was expected to make a comeback, and it did. State lawmakers are once again faced with a contentious bill that stirred up fierce debate last session.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In rural America, scarce doctors battle misinformation as they practice medicine
item.description: Conspiracy theories about health fill a vacuum created by the lack of doctors in many rural communities. Meanwhile, doctors in these areas say patients have become increasingly distrustful and sometimes hostile.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court hears case that questions major plank of voting rights
item.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday hears a case that could strike down the last major part of the 1965 Voting Rights Act that remains standing.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg and Alyssa Kapasi, MPR News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Katie Porter expresses regret for tense exchange with reporter, berating staffer
item.description: Former California congresswoman Katie Porter is attempting to press "reset" on her campaign for governor following a disastrous week that started with a video of a tense exchange with a journalist and then evolved into a broader critique of her temperament when another video surfaced.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Grand jury proceedings and subpoenas surround Hope Florida
item.description: Hope Florida, a program aiming to help folks get off government assistance, has faced scrutiny this past year over a mysterious money trail that started with a Medicaid settlement and ended with a political action committee.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
